official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript v2 DowntimesApi.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: exact operations and statuses: POST /api/v2/downtime with application/json returning 200; DELETE /api/v2/downtime/{downtime_id} returning 204 with no body; auth via apiKeyAuth (DD-API-KEY) and appKeyAuth (DD-APPLICATION-KEY); errors deserialized from the APIErrorResponse {errors: [string]} envelope on 400/403/404/429

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript DowntimeCreateRequestAttributes.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: create attributes: scope and monitor_identifier required; optional message, mute_first_recovery_notification, notify_end_states, notify_end_types, display_timezone, schedule

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript DowntimeScheduleOneTimeCreateUpdateRequest.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: one-time schedule: start and end are ISO-8601 datetimes that must include a UTC offset of zero; omitted start starts immediately and omitted end continues forever

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript DowntimeScheduleRecurrenceCreateUpdateRequest.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: recurring schedule entries: rrule and duration required (duration is an integer plus m/h/d/w), start is ISO-8601 and must NOT include a UTC offset

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript DowntimeRelationships.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: downtime response relationships: monitor (the monitor identified by the downtime) and created_by (the user who created the downtime), each a JSON:API relationship whose data carries id and resource type

research.language_rationale: Our deploy tooling that plans maintenance windows is TypeScript on Node. Datadog's official datadog-api-client-typescript is a generated wrapper over these same v2 endpoints, and the verify environment is offline with no npm installs, so the built-in fetch against the documented v2 JSON:API contract is the credible integration surface; the official client sources double as the authoritative wire reference.

verified_facts: Creating a downtime is POST /api/v2/downtime with a JSON:API resource document {data: {type: 'downtime', attributes}}; success is 200.
Canceling is DELETE /api/v2/downtime/{downtime_id}; success is 204 with no response body, and 404 signals the downtime no longer exists.
Both operations authenticate with DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY headers.
scope and monitor_identifier are the required create attributes; monitor_identifier is a strict union of {monitor_id} or {monitor_tags}.
One-time schedules take start/end ISO-8601 datetimes that must include a zero UTC offset; omitting start means start now and omitting end means run forever.
Recurring schedules take recurrences of {rrule, duration, start?} plus an optional timezone; duration is an integer with an m/h/d/w suffix and the recurrence start must not include a UTC offset.
The response document carries data.id plus relationships: monitor (data null for tag-scoped downtimes, otherwise {id, type: 'monitors'}) and created_by ({id, type: 'users'}).
Errors on 400/403/404/429 use the APIErrorResponse envelope {errors: [string]}.
